response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to be vigilant and critical of the information shared. Here are some steps you can take: 1) Verify the authenticity of the source by checking if the news is reported by reputable sources or not. 2) Look for fact-checking websites that can help you identify misinformation. 3) Be cautious of sensationalized headlines and articles that seem too good (or bad) to be true. 4) Consider multiple perspectives before forming an opinion. 5) Use privacy settings on social media platforms to control what type of content you see. By following these steps, you can make informed decisions and avoid falling for fake information. Remember, it's always better to be safe than sorry when it comes to managing and dealing with fake content on social media."
,"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is essential to be vigilant and critical of the information shared. One should verify the authenticity of the source by checking if the news is reported by reputable sources or not. Additionally, looking for fact-checking websites can help identify misinformation. Furthermore, being cautious of sensationalized headlines and articles that seem too good (or bad) to be true is crucial. Considering multiple perspectives before forming an opinion is also important. Finally, using privacy settings on social media platforms can control what type of content one sees."

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to be vigilant and critical of the information shared. Here are some steps you can take: 1) Verify the authenticity of the source by checking if the news is reported by reputable sources or not. 2) Look for fact-checking websites that can help you identify misinformation. 3) Be cautious of sensationalized headlines and articles that seem too good (or bad) to be true. 4) Consider multiple perspectives before forming an opinion. 5) Use privacy settings on social media platforms to control what type of content you see. By following these steps, you can make informed decisions and avoid falling for fake information. Remember, it's always better to be safe than sorry when it comes to managing and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is essential to be vigilant and critical of the information shared. One should verify the authenticity of the source by checking if the news is reported by reputable sources or not. Additionally, looking for fact-checking websites can help identify misinformation. Furthermore, being cautious of sensationalized headlines and articles that seem too good (or bad) to be true is crucial. Considering multiple perspectives before forming an opinion is also important. Finally, using privacy settings on social media platforms can control what type of content one sees.
